title: C. elegans bleaching solution preparation
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"au8v","text":"The C. elegans bleaching solution is used for several purposes: ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":4,"length":10}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"bjmrq","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"392cl","text":"- destroying and removing bacterial or fungal cells from a contaminated C. elegans population. This is possible because C. elegans eggs are resistant to the bleaching used in this protocol but most fungi and bacteria are not.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":72,"length":10},{"style":"italic","offset":120,"length":10}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"fi7c0","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"anhbk","text":"- synchronising a C. elegans population consisting of worms at different developmental stages. This is possible because eggs are resistant to the bleaching used in this protocol but hatched worms (larvae and adults) are not.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":18,"length":10}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"2jp46","text":"The bleaching solution can be kept at room temperature for one month.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"120fo","text":"Prepare the bleaching solution.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"956b7","text":"Get a plate of gravid (= pregnant) worms or a plate with lots of unhatched eggs if you want to carry out an egg prep.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"emdd7","text":"Sodium hypochlorite and sodium hydoxide are highly toxic and should be handled with care, wearing a lab coat and goggles. Read relevant information.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Add 42 ml DEPC water to a 50 ml Falcon tube.
See relevant protocol for details but 6 ml of bleaching solution for 5 minutes on a vortex in 14 ml tubes should be sufficient to dissolve worms and spare eggs.
The bleaching solution can be kept at room temperature for one month.
creator: Cristian Riccio
